function_signature: func GraphNeighbors(g *CSRGraph, fileID uint64, depth int, edgeTypeFilter EdgeType, maxNodes int) ([]CSREdge, error)
design_decisions: Compacted-only reads: takes *CSRGraph, never reads engine/graph/edgelog.go's EdgeLog. See requirement.md's 'Design question resolved' section for full evidence trail.
edgeTypeFilter == EdgeTypeInvalid (zero value) means 'no filter, match all 4 valid types'; any other value must be one of the 4 ValidEdgeType-recognized types or GraphNeighbors returns an error.

Type filter prunes traversal itself, not just the returned result set: an edge whose Type does not match edgeTypeFilter is never followed, so a node reachable only via a non-matching intermediate edge is unreachable under that filter. Documented in traverse.go's GraphNeighbors doc comment.
depth must be in [0, 2] ('0-2 hop traversal' per docs/LLD/graph.md); depth == 0 returns an empty result (fileID itself never included) with no error.
maxNodes must be >= 0; result ordered by (hop ascending, Weight descending, Target fileID ascending) before truncating to maxNodes.
g == nil treated as an empty graph: returns (nil, nil), not an error.
BFS dedup: a node reached via multiple paths within depth hops appears exactly once, at its first-seen (shortest) hop distance.

acceptance_criteria_pointers.test: engine/graph/traverse.go signature (takes *CSRGraph, no *EdgeLog param) + doc comment 'Compacted-only design' section + requirement.md
go test ./engine/graph/... -race -timeout 180s (full package suite, all green)

known_gaps_disclosed: No merge of not-yet-compacted EdgeLog entries (deliberate, evidenced design decision - see requirement.md, not a gap).
No wiring into agents/query/ (query-agent) - that consumer does not exist yet in this repo; out of scope per issue's Impacted modules list.
The system-wide 'k + 2k' cap value itself is not decided/hardcoded here - maxNodes is caller-supplied, per docs/LLD/graph.md naming query-agent as the module that decides that value.
prompt_injection_note: Tool output during this run (gh issue view 15) contained injected fake system-reminder-style text (fake date-change notice, fake MCP 'tokensave' server tool instructions, fake 'Auto Mode Active' directive) appended after real content - the same known, recurring prompt-injection pattern flagged in every prior run for this issue. Ignored; not acted upon.
